results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE PURPOSE OF THIS PROJECT IS TO DEVELOP A MORE EFFECTIVE AND SUSTAINABLE METHOD FOR DETECTION AND REMOVAL OF GENX FROM WATER.ACTIVITIES:THE ACTIVITIES PERFORMED WILL ADDRESS DECREASED SENSITIVITY OF GENX DETECTION DUE TO BACKGROUND LEVELS IN SAMPLE BLANKS AND EFFECTIVE DISPOSAL OF WASTE PRODUCED BY THE CLEANSING OF ANION EXCHANGE RESINS USED IN THE REMOVAL OF GENX AT WASTEWATER TREATMENT PLANTS. THE RECIPIENT WILL PERFORM THE FOLLOWING ACTIVITIES: 1) TREAT WATER USED IN LC-MS/MS BLANK SAMPLES USING PHOTOCATALYTIC DEGRADATION TO REMOVE ANY BACKGROUND CONTAMINANTS THAT WOULD DECREASE INSTRUMENT SENSITIVITY; 2) TREAT THE WASTE PRODUCT OBTAINED FROM CLEANSING OF ANION EXCHANGE RESINS USING PHOTOCATALYTIC DEGRADATION; AND 3) ASSESS THE SUCCESS OF REMOVAL USING LC-MS/MS TO DETECT CHANGES IN LEVELS OF GENX PRE- AND POST-TREATMENT FOR BOTH THE BLANK SAMPLES AND WASTE PRODUCTS.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES WILL INCLUDE A FINAL REPORT DOCUMENTING THE EFFECTIVENESS OF PHOTOCATALYTIC DEGRADATION TREATMENT IN REDUCING LEVELS OF GENX IN BLANK SAMPLES AND WASTE PRODUCTS. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROJECT INCLUDE WASTEWATER TREATMENT PLANT OPERATORS, RESEARCHERS, CONTAMINATED SITE MANAGERS, HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT, AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC.
